features.description: User authentication with role-based access for field operators
steps: Open app and view login screen
Enter valid operator credentials
Verify successful login and redirection to Operator Dashboard
Verify JWT token contains id_empresa, rol (OPERADOR), id_usuario

features.description: Daily Equipment Report (Parte Diario) creation and submission
steps: Navigate to 'New Daily Report' from Dashboard
Select assigned equipment from dropdown
Enter start and end hours/odometer readings
Enter work activities performed
Attach Fuel Voucher (if applicable)
Sign the report digitally
Submit the report
Verify submission success and report appears in history

features.description: Daily Report triggers Structured Approval Template
steps: Submit a Daily Report
Verify backend API is called to instantiate the approval workflow (e.g., Operator -> Supervisor -> Resident Engineer)
Verify the report status changes to 'Pending Approval'
Verify the report appears in the 'Enviados' (Sent) tab of the Approvals Hub
Navigate to 'Fuel Vouchers' module
Enter voucher number and fuel quantity
Take a photo of the physical voucher
Save the voucher record
Verify the voucher can be selected when creating a Daily Report
Navigate to 'Checklists'
Select equipment and checklist type (Daily/Weekly)
Go through inspection items and pass/fail each
Add comments or photos for failed items
Submit checklist
Verify checklist is saved and failed items trigger a notification to Maintenance
Navigate to 'Approvals' from the main menu
View the 'Recibidos' (Received) tab
Verify received items list pending requests requiring the user's approval
View the 'Enviados' (Sent) tab
Verify sent items list requests initiated by the user with their current status (Pending at Step X, Approved, Rejected)
Navigate to Approvals Hub and click 'New Ad-Hoc Request'
Enter Title and Description
Attach supporting documents or images
Manually select specific users as Approvers
Manually select specific users as CC/Informed
Submit the request
Verify the request appears in the 'Enviados' tab and triggers the backend API
Open a pending request from the 'Recibidos' tab
Review request details, attachments, and current step
Add an optional comment
Click 'Approve' or 'Reject'
Verify request disappears from 'Recibidos' and backend API is updated

features.description: Offline mode for Daily Reports and Checklists
steps: Turn off device network connection
Create and save a Daily Report
Create and save a Checklist
Verify records are saved locally with 'Pending Sync' status
Turn on network connection
Verify records sync to backend and status updates to 'Submitted'

features.description: Mobile App is an isolated project within the Monorepo
steps: Verify the Flutter app is created in a dedicated directory (e.g., /mobile or /apps/mobile)
Verify the mobile app only interacts with the backend via REST API
Verify no direct cross-dependencies or modifications infect the existing Angular frontend or Node.js backend codebases to support the mobile app, except for necessary API endpoints

features.description: Mobile forms follow Aero Design System layout rules
steps: Open any form (e.g., Daily Report, Checklist)
Verify single-column layout is used
Verify vertical gap between fields is 24px (var(--s-24))
Verify primary action button is placed at the top of vertical stacks or bottom right for dialogs

features.description: Consistent UI components matching Aero design widgets
steps: Navigate through app screens
Verify buttons match <aero-button> styling (primary-500 for interactive)
Verify inputs match <aero-input> and <aero-dropdown> aesthetics
Verify cards match <aero-card> aesthetics
Verify text uses Universal Sans equivalent or system sans-serif with correct hierarchy

features.description: Color palette strictly follows Bitcorp Aero tokens
steps: Inspect app visual elements
Verify primary text is #072b45 (primary-900)
Verify interactive elements are #0077cd (primary-500)
Verify app background is #ebf3f8 (primary-100)
Verify error states use #d10031 (semantic-red-500)

features.description: Modals and Dialogs adhere to Aero guidelines
steps: Trigger a destructive action or confirmation
Verify app uses a custom styled Aero modal/dialog instead of native OS alerts
Verify primary action in modal is at the bottom right

features.description: Responsive layout adapts to standard Android cellphones and tablets
steps: Open app on a standard Android cellphone (e.g., 360px - 412px width)
Verify UI elements, forms, and lists fit comfortably without horizontal scrolling
Open app on an Android tablet (e.g., 768px - 800px width)
Verify UI takes advantage of larger screen real estate (e.g., multi-column forms where appropriate or wider modal dialogs)
Rotate device between portrait and landscape
Verify UI responds dynamically using Flutter's LayoutBuilder and MediaQuery

features.description: Application targets mobile/field users on Android devices primarily
steps: Verify the Flutter project is configured to target Android (targetSdkVersion, compileSdkVersion)
Verify UI elements are sized for touch accessibility (minimum touch targets mapping to Android Material Design/Aero standards)
Verify core mobile features (camera, offline storage) are prioritized in the plugin configurations
